branch.conclusion: Delegate to tts-basics synthesize(). TTSService chunking overhead is unjustified for short payloads. Cache key still includes provider.

branch.question: Does the full-payload cache exist at `{cache_dir}/{full_key}/assembled.mp3`?

branch.conclusion: Return cached path + chunks index. Skip synth. Still run eviction at end to keep total size under cap.

branch.question: Is the consumer a file (cache_dir target) or a real-time stream (WebSocket / pyaudio)?

branch.conclusion: Use stream_tts() async generator. Bridge to sync via asyncio.run(collect_stream()). Never call stream_to_file from inside async without to_thread().

branch.question: Is the voice mode library (pre-existing voice id) or clone (new voice from samples)?
Is there a valid consent record for this speaker matching the requested scope?

branch.conclusion: Reject: return status="error", error="missing consent". Do NOT call clone_voice. Surface to human for consent collection.
Proceed with ElevenLabs clone_voice. Attach consent_id to output. Cache key uses cloned voice_id.
Standard long-form path: tempfile.mkdtemp() → semantic_split() → parallel synth → assemble → cache → evict. Apply rule r4 split discipline.

example.p: Input: 18000-char article, voice=onyx, model=tts-1-hd, library voice, deliver as file. char_count > 4000 → cache miss → file delivery → library voice → conclusion r3-chunk-tempdir-isolation: mkdtemp, 5 chunks, parallel synth under semaphore, pydub assemble, cache + evict.
